commit.message:
fix: replace raw S3 PUT with properly signed rust-s3 client

The previous upload_to_s3() did a raw HTTP PUT without AWS Signature V4,
making access_key/secret_key completely unused. This always 403'd on real
S3 buckets.

Now uses rust-s3 crate with proper authentication:
- Full SigV4 signing via rust-s3 Bucket::put_object_with_content_type()
- Supports S3-compatible services (R2, MinIO, Spaces, etc.)
- New optional settings: s3_custom_domain (CDN URL), s3_path_style (MinIO)
- Proper Content-Type detection preserved
- Correct error reporting with status codes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(formatter): handle PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY without warning (#3)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Fixes two bugs in the PIVOT + ORDER BY workaround:

**Bug 1 — multi-statement files only formatted the first tree**

Files with multiple statements (e.g. two `SET VARIABLE` statements
followed by a `PIVOT`) produce multiple parse trees. The workaround
previously only formatted `trees[0]`, leaving preceding statements
unformatted. Now all trees are formatted; the ORDER BY clause is
appended only to the last one (the PIVOT).

**Bug 2 — PIVOT subquery stripped table qualifiers from column
references**

Root cause: DuckDB's generator `TRANSFORMS` maps `exp.Pivot` to a
`preprocess([unqualify_columns])` transform. This runs before generation
and calls `.pop()` on the table/db/catalog parts of every `Column` node
inside the PIVOT, turning `sc.sku_key` into `sku_key`. Even though the
AST was correct, generation always produced unqualified names.

Fix: override `TRANSFORMS` in `JarifyGenerator` to exclude the
`exp.Pivot` entry. The dispatch then falls through to `pivot_sql`
directly — no column modification — and qualifiers are preserved
verbatim.

## Testing

- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/pivot_order_by.expected.sql` regenerated with
correct qualified output
- 46/46 tests passing

Resolves #2

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(tree-agent): support staged allowed types in schema type generation (#26960)

## Description

Adds support for staged allowed types in the tree-agent's TypeScript
schema rendering. Staged types are types that can be read from a node
but cannot yet be written —
they represent a forward-compatible schema upgrade that has not been
fully committed.

Previously, the type generator treated all allowed types uniformly for
reads and writes. With this change:

- Array and map nodes with staged types render as TreeArray<TRead,
TWrite> / TreeMap<TRead, TWrite>, where TRead includes staged types and
TWrite excludes them.
- Object node fields with staged types render as getter/setter pairs
(get foo(): string | number; set foo(value: string);) instead of simple
properties, enforcing
  write-time restrictions.
- Record nodes include a warning comment noting which types are staged
and not writeable.
- The TreeArray and TreeMap prompt documentation is updated to reflect
the dual type parameter pattern.

## Reviewer Guidance

The review process is outlined on [this wiki
page](https://github.com/microsoft/FluidFramework/wiki/PR-Guidelines#guidelines).

---------

Forms: Fix fatal error when field render value is an array (#48032)

* Fix fatal error in get_legacy_extra_values when field value is an array

Guard against array values being used as array keys in the
get_legacy_extra_values method. When a form field (e.g., email) receives
array POST data, get_render_value() returns an array which cannot be
used as a PHP array key, causing a TypeError fatal error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enforce string values for single-value field types

For field types that should always be strings (email, name, url,
subject, textarea, ip), take the first element when array POST data
is submitted. Also use first-element fallback in get_legacy_extra_values
as a defensive guard for any pre-existing array values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite test to reproduce the fatal without test isolation dependency

Use direct Feedback_Field construction with array value for a url-type
field and v2 format storage, which works in isolation. The key is using
'submit' context — get_render_submit_value() returns the raw array,
unlike 'default' which implodes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add parameterized tests for all non-extra field types with array values

Test every field type in $non_extra_fields (email, name, url, subject,
textarea, ip) with array values across both submit and default contexts.
Also verify checkbox-multiple arrays are preserved in submit context
and imploded in default context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Strengthen test assertions to verify data correctness, not just no-crash

Assert that extra values contain expected field values (Comment text
field in parameterized tests, checkbox-multiple array in preservation
test), not just that the method returns an array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove workaround for dotnet/sdk#52160

The .NET SDK fix (dotnet/sdk#52161) is available since SDK 10.0.105.
The workaround in PublishChiseledContainer that manually appended
'-extra' to ContainerFamily when InvariantGlobalization=false is no
longer needed - the SDK now handles this correctly.

UpdateContainerBaseImage now extracts the container family from the
already-computed ContainerBaseImage tag instead of using the raw
ContainerFamily property. This ensures CustomContainerBaseImageVersion
preserves the correct family suffix (e.g. noble-chiseled-extra) as
resolved by the SDK, since the SDK task only outputs ContainerBaseImage
and does not update ContainerFamily.

Test updated to explicitly set InvariantGlobalization=true for the
non-extra variant, reflecting the SDK's new default behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Specify default number of values (9) for breakdown settings in Lens charts (#5810)

The "Number of values" setting across all Lens chart types didn't
document its default. The default was recently increased to 9 (from 3 or
5 depending on dimension position) per elastic/kibana#247015 — already
live in Serverless, coming in Stack 9.4.

- Added version-specific default values using structured bullet lists
with `{applies_to}` tags (not inline) to all 18 occurrences of the
"Number of values" setting across 12 chart type docs: line, bar, area,
table, metric, pie, heat map, waffle, tag cloud, mosaic, treemap, region
map
- Used precise old defaults per dimension position based on Kibana
source code logic (`previousBucketsLength === 0 ? 5 : DEFAULT_SIZE`
where `DEFAULT_SIZE = 3`):
- **5** for 12 primary/sole bucket dimensions: horizontal axis
(line/bar/area/heat-map/mosaic), rows (tables), and single dimensions
(pie/metric/waffle/tag-cloud/treemap/region-map)
- **3** for 6 secondary/breakdown dimensions: breakdown (line/bar/area),
split metrics by (tables), vertical axis (heat-map/mosaic)
- Tutorial/how-to examples that explicitly set specific values (e.g.
`4`, `10`) are unchanged — those are intentional user-chosen values, not
defaults

Example of the change pattern:

```markdown
# Before
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display.

# After (primary dimension)
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display. The default number of values depends on your environment:
  - {applies_to}`serverless: ga` {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.4` Defaults to 9.
  - {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.0-9.3` Defaults to 5.

# After (breakdown/secondary dimension)
- **Number of values**: How many top values to display. The default number of values depends on your environment:
  - {applies_to}`serverless: ga` {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.4` Defaults to 9.
  - {applies_to}`stack: ga 9.0-9.3` Defaults to 3.
```

---------

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput (#226)

* 🎨 Palette: Add clear action to SearchInput

This change adds a "Clear" button to the `SearchInput` component that appears when the input is not empty.

Key improvements:
- Added `RiCloseLine` button to clear the input immediately.
- The clear action triggers the `onChange` callback immediately, bypassing the debounce delay for better responsiveness.
- Added `aria-label` for accessibility.
- Adjusted input padding to prevent text overlap with the clear button.
- Verified visually with Playwright and ensured the project builds correctly.

Co-authored-by: jakwakwa <20184974+jakwakwa@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

`handleClear` calls `onChange("")` immediately, but the existing debounced `useEffect` will also call `onChange(query)` after the debounce delay when `query` becomes empty. This guarantees a second `onChange("")` emission on every clear, which can cause duplicate work for consumers that trigger side effects directly in `onChange` (e.g., network requests). Consider canceling/skipping the pending debounced emission on clear (e.g., switch to a debounced callback you can `cancel()`, or guard the effect with a ref to ignore the next debounced update after a manual clear).

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🎨 Palette: Refine SearchInput clear button and remove log

- Improved clear button size and padding for better UX.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(serializer): add missing return after free on corrupted RDB buffer (#1843)

* fix(serializer): add missing return after free on corrupted RDB buffer

In RdbLoadDeletedNodes and RdbLoadDeletedEdges (v18, v19), a misaligned
buffer triggers rm_free() followed by ASSERT(false). In release builds
ASSERT compiles to a no-op, so execution falls through to use the freed
pointer (use-after-free) and free it again (double-free).

Add explicit return statements after rm_free() in all four error paths
so the functions exit immediately regardless of build configuration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(serializer): add unit tests for deleted-entity buffer validation

Tests misaligned and aligned buffers for RdbLoadDeletedNodes_v19 and
RdbLoadDeletedEdges_v19. The misaligned tests verify the early-return
path does not cause use-after-free or double-free. The aligned tests
verify the happy path correctly marks entities as deleted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(serializer): skip misaligned buffer tests in debug builds

In debug builds, ASSERT(false) intentionally crashes (NULL deref) before
the return statement is reached, so the misaligned buffer tests can only
exercise the early-return path in release builds. Wrap them with
#if !RG_DEBUG guards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(serializer): use RedisModule_Assert for malformed RDB buffers

Replace silent return with RedisModule_Log + RedisModule_Assert(false) to
notify Redis and abort on corrupted deleted-entity buffers. Also validates
buffer count matches expected count (not just alignment).

Extends fix to v17 decoder which had no validation in release builds.
Converts unit tests to fork-based death tests to verify abort behavior.

Addresses review comment: use assert+exit instead of silent return, handle
short read / malformed RDB and notify Redis.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(test): use _exit in mock assert to prevent ASAN hang after fork

The mock RedisModule__Assert in death tests was a no-op, letting
exit(1) from RedisModule_Assert run ASAN atexit handlers in the
forked child. This hangs because ASAN tries to clean up thread-local
state that does not exist after fork(). Using _exit(1) in the mock
bypasses atexit handlers entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* removed unit test

commit.message:
Forms: Fix fatal error when field render value is an array (#48032)

* Fix fatal error in get_legacy_extra_values when field value is an array

Guard against array values being used as array keys in the
get_legacy_extra_values method. When a form field (e.g., email) receives
array POST data, get_render_value() returns an array which cannot be
used as a PHP array key, causing a TypeError fatal error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enforce string values for single-value field types

For field types that should always be strings (email, name, url,
subject, textarea, ip), take the first element when array POST data
is submitted. Also use first-element fallback in get_legacy_extra_values
as a defensive guard for any pre-existing array values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rewrite test to reproduce the fatal without test isolation dependency

Use direct Feedback_Field construction with array value for a url-type
field and v2 format storage, which works in isolation. The key is using
'submit' context — get_render_submit_value() returns the raw array,
unlike 'default' which implodes it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add parameterized tests for all non-extra field types with array values

Test every field type in $non_extra_fields (email, name, url, subject,
textarea, ip) with array values across both submit and default contexts.
Also verify checkbox-multiple arrays are preserved in submit context
and imploded in default context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Strengthen test assertions to verify data correctness, not just no-crash

Assert that extra values contain expected field values (Comment text
field in parameterized tests, checkbox-multiple array in preservation
test), not just that the method returns an array.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix changelog entry: add trailing period and broaden description to cover both fixed sites

* fix: rebuild images with proper version tag in release workflow

Replace imagetools re-tagging with actual Docker builds using the
release version tag. The CI layer cache means only the final VERSION
layer changes (~1s per image). Also builds plugins with the release
version ldflags instead of downloading from the CI run (which had

The release workflow now has:
- version: detect release merge, extract version from CHANGELOG
- build (matrix): rebuild images per-arch with proper VERSION arg
- manifest: create multi-arch manifests from per-arch images
- release: tag, render manifests, build plugins, SBOM, draft release

commit.message:
feat(formatter): semicolons on own line, AS alignment, graceful parse errors (#1)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR description was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Three formatter style fixes from real-world usage feedback.

### Trailing semicolons on their own line
Previously `;` was appended to the last line of each statement. Now it
always appears as a standalone line.

### Column alias AS alignment
When a SELECT has 2+ aliased columns (all single-line), the `AS`
keywords are padded to align vertically:

```sql
SELECT
   first_name    AS first
  ,last_name     AS last
  ,email_address AS email
FROM t
```

Multi-line column expressions (e.g. `CASE`) disable alignment for the
whole SELECT.

### Graceful handling of parse failures
`format_sql` now returns `(formatted_sql, warnings)` instead of
crashing. On `ParseError`, the original SQL is returned unchanged with a
warning. This partially addresses #2 (`PIVOT ... USING ... ORDER BY ...`
not yet supported by sqlglot) — the formatter no longer crashes; a
proper fix is tracked separately.

## Changes

- `formatter.py` — returns `(str, list[FormatWarning])` tuple; catches
`ParseError` gracefully
- `generator.py` — adds `alias_sql` override with `_as_align_width`
state for AS alignment
- `cli.py` — unpacks the new tuple, prints warnings as `WARN` instead of
crashing
- All callers updated to unpack the tuple
- 4 new tests; 44/44 passing
- `tmp/` added to `.gitignore`

Resolves #2

---------

Add Content Editor: AI-powered suggestions, drafts, and content plans

New 'Content Editor' view in the sidebar with three capabilities:

1. **New Content suggestions** — identifies uncovered documentation topics
   grouped by category, with priority badges and topic counts
2. **Update recommendations** — flags partially-matched modules that need
   content expansion, showing match scores
3. **Outdated module detection** — surfaces modules >12 months old that
   need refreshing

Each suggestion has a 'Generate Draft' button that uses GitHub Models AI to
produce:
- For new modules: complete index.yml draft with title, summary, learning
  objectives, units, prerequisites, and suggested repo placement
- For updates: specific content changes, new units, and revised objectives
- For outdated: staleness assessment, rewrite plan, and refresh checklist

Additional features:
- 'Content Plan' button generates a full prioritized plan (immediate /
  short-term / long-term) exportable as Markdown
- Draft preview panel with markdown rendering
